item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No. That was almost certainly not the intention.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I’m honestly so excited to see this thing in action in Containment Breach Unity. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
No personnel are to listen to SCP-058 for more than thirty (30) minutes at a time.

Why? There's no mention of an auditory hazard. This looks like a missed remnant from a past edit.

In case of escape, facility is to be considered compromised and detonation of on-site nuclear weaponry is to commence.

There's really not enough explanation of how strong he is. He's hostile and violent, strong, and fast, but none of that explains why he can't be incapacitated. Able is also hostile and strong, but he can usually be killed with the on-site nuke being only a last-resort. Does 058 survive a nuke? Nobody knows, because the info isn't there. Is he stronger than Able? Well, it's implied at best.

This shows all the symptoms of an early SCP made when the standards were bare minimal and the cryptic poetry hadn't yet been overdone.

item.title: Cause of Death: Heart Attack
item.content:encoded: obligatory bad pun

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don’t think SCP-redacted is necessarily an apocalyptic being by itself. The redacted event is some big natural disaster that happens when one of the Scarlet King’s seals break. 2317 says the chains are made from the bones of other devourer entities; I think when 231 gives birth those entities are reincarnated and the chain breaks as result. As for what the kid actually is, we literally know nothing other than the fact that it fits inside a human uterus at some point of its life cycle.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It was but they also noted that they killed the results as well, with increasing difficulty. They could be lying of course but 231 entities are generally framed as potentially world ending and 58 is not that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wasn't that referring to the brides themselves?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm not so sure. I'm pretty sure in SCP-231 it states that all SCP-██ instances die soon after they're born.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"SCP-058 was first encountered at Site-██ as it came out of [DATA EXPUNGED]."

"SCP-058 was first encountered at Site-██████ as it came out of SCP-231?"

item.title: MOD POST - INFO - OPEN
item.content:encoded:
I'd like to point out, that given that section of the skip was more imagery and prose than just regular text, that may have been an intentional grammatic dissonance.

"queen" could have been an odd adjective for effect rather than a typo.

I'm not taking any action at this point, but I'm gonna consult with a couple of other staff, and see what they think.

EDIT:

I've reverted the change to "queen wonders".

  • A peer on one of the crit teams said they felt that queen wonders may have been intentional.
  • I also felt that "queen wonders" may have been intentional.
  • A fellow moderator felt that it could have been either a typo or intentional, and thus we err on the side of authorial intent.

Noting this isn't a disciplinary action in any way, and no one is being yelled at.

Thanks for your attention to keeping the wiki quality! <3

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You're right, sorry if I stepped on your toes Baron.

It's alright. I was just explaining the reasoning why.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I thought the sounding off was intentional, though I guess there isn't really any way to know without asking the author, who seems to be AWOL at the moment. You're right, sorry if I stepped on your toes Baron.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I also preferred “queen wonders” to “queen’s wonders,” and I think that might count as a content edit but it wasn’t done by the author.

It is a grammatical edit. It doesn't significantly change anything. Also, the sentence sounds off for it clearly looks like it is suppose to be a possessive. It just sounds off.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It seems like “widespread” should be “wide spread”, based on context. “A wide spread of stinger fluid” makes a lot more sense than “a widespread of stinger fluid”.

I also preferred “queen wonders” to “queen’s wonders,” and I think that might count as a content edit but it wasn’t done by the author.

Edit: Looking through the page history, when the phrase was first added by Gears it was "wide spread". Since this is a grammatical correction, I'm gonna go ahead and change it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The scary thing about this bad boy is the disconnect between its "thing-what-kills-you" body and its word-salad-producing mind. What kind of motives does a thing like this have? What kind of mind? Also,

“SCP-058 was first encountered at Site ██ as it came out of [DATA EXPUNGED].”

“SCP-058 was first encountered at Site 54 as it came out of SCP-1983-1.”

In that case, maybe the voice of 058 is the leader of the cult that created 1983.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
“SCP-058 was first encountered at Site ██ as it came out of [DATA EXPUNGED].”

“SCP-058 was first encountered at Site 54 as it came out of SCP-1983-1.”

That's my theory, anyway; it seems to fit.

item.title: Re: Just wondering...
item.content:encoded: The resections lead me to believe that it becomes bloated and immobile after eating, thereby making it easier to contain.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: They feed it because it bloats up and stops moving, I think.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Does anyone else feel like the nuclear response in the containment procedures is a bit much? It doesn't feel like this thing would be hard to find or hard to kill with conventional weaponry.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This ones awesome. its nice to know not everything needs telepathy to kill you but it feels kind of weird with the doctor saying "let him go" or whatever it was. given its the foundation shouldnt he be asking something like, "what do you mean" or "what do you want"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A very fascinating SCP and one of my all time favorites. I like how it has some ambiguity in regards to it's origins which can be anything, leading to many interesting interpretations. The best part is the Lovecraftian poetry it speaks while murdering people. A fun read.

item.title: Re: Why is this so popular?
item.content:encoded: They feed it because it gets bloated and doesn't move afterward. Assuming that the redactions in the article refer to it eating people.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is one of those SCPs that are simultaneously terrifying and ridiculous and I love it!

One of my favorite SCPs and one of the first ones I've been exposed to!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is scary and intense

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This SCP to me is creepy as hell but also so interesting. I really want to know what these poems mean, and why are they being spoken by this heart? Where is it getting these poems from or is it making them up? Although it may just be some mombo jumbo but still pretty cool.

item.title: Re: Image?
item.content:encoded: The third image is laughably bad.

item.title: Re: Image?
item.content:encoded: There's three image files attached to this page, did you look at them?

item.title: Image?
item.content:encoded:
So I heard a bunch about this thing once having an image.

Now I wasn't around for when this thing did have an image, and when I tried comparing the versions using the History option, none of them had the image.

Anyone still have it? It's something I wouldn't mind seeing. Even if it is, apparently a load of crap.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love the romantic word salad this thing spews forth continuously. I think it was creatively written as well. Imagining this monstrosity ceaselessly projecting poetry in such a calm tone (I hear it as calm, deep, and monotone. Audible from every direction, as though by an invisible loudspeaker around the SCP) … an awesome contrast to its freakishly violent behavior!

I wanted to give this SCP a +1 so much that I made an account!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Regarding the discovery log of this thing being vague, I've generally interpreted the [DATA EXPUNGED] at a Foundation site to imply it actually just turned out to have been the heart of someone/something on-site when it came out and starting killing people.

No-voting for now since it is "just" a monster what kills you by modern standards, but I've always kinda liked how bizarre this one is.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I will just quote Zyn above me here:

According to the page history, this article was created on 25 Jul 2008.

That means it's had almost eight years to accumulate upvotes, starting when standards were a lot lower than they are today.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't understand the high rating on this one.

It's an evil unkillable thing, is that scary? i can see how it's meant to be scary, but the concept is kind of overused. i could swear i saw ten others like it just reading here from the start of the archive.

The details of the containment are pointlessly explicit, theres guidelines about too much detail.

My biggest issue though is with the end. It seems like the author just pulled things out of a random word generator, its just line after line of nonsequiturs

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: To be fair Jacob, that sentence about SCP-058 is likely a reference to SCP-231.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You can just look at the up/downvotes on the article.

I specifically used the adjusted rating because you are saying that this article is "bad" by current standards. If that were true then it should be rated negatively by a majority of currently active users, but with its currently positive adjusted rating (that is pretty high even compared to many successful recent articles) it seems inaccurate to say that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: scpper gives the number adjusted for people who are currently active. That can make a massive difference with older articles- sometimes it can even push an apparently well-rated article into the negatives.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You can just look at the up/downvotes on the article

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yes, but that's only if you post something that is "bad" by today's standards.

If you post something good by today's standards, it will accumulate upvotes and survive. But if you come back to it eight years later, it may be the case that tastes have changed, and new users looking at your article for the first time would say that it is not quality by any means.

Think about it this way:

If Apple released the original iPhone today, no-one would buy it, because it's not "a quality [phone] by any means" in today's market. But 9 years ago, when it was first released, it was the best thing on the market. Technology, and consumer expectation, has moved on. That doesn't diminish the significance of the iPhone as a product, or render foolish the people who bought it in 2007.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Also, for me at least, this is an example of "a monster what kills a bunch of people" done right- it's not presented as a thing we should be terrified of, but as a really, really frigg'n weird thing that happens to be kind of dangerous. It's a cool monster, yes, but there's more to it than that.

Note, for instance, that the containment procedures are just "put it in a really strong box, inspect the box regularly, and feed it a cow every couple days". In a pinch they'll happily just kill it. They're keeping it alive because they want to record what it says. Notice that line about limiting exposure to said recordings, too. That, combined with the [Data Expunged] on where it came from, nicely imply that this thing is part of a meta-anomaly… and that it might be the part that makes sense by comparison. That's what makes this thing interesting to me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: http://scpper.com has this at +146 (+166/-20) adjusted for currently active users only. It seems to be reasonably successful even with the present audience.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
True, but what constitutes a "bad article" will change over time.

Back when this was first posted, this was considered a good article. Standards have changed over the years, so the age on this one shows. I'll admit, though, that there are other Series I articles that are worse than this one.

There are others that have seen standards change and end up in negative territory before, and they are either deleted or rewritten. If it's popular in some way, there will be significant interest in rewriting it - and it's then you'll see the improvements.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: But if I post a bad article and come back to it eight years later, it won't have "accumulated upvotes", it will accumulate downvotes and then get deleted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
How does this have so many upvotes? It's not even a quality SCP by any means.

According to the page history, this article was created on 25 Jul 2008.

That means it's had almost eight years to accumulate upvotes, starting when standards were a lot lower than they are today.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
How does this have so many upvotes? It's not even a quality SCP by any means.

It's just "it's a big creepy pulsing heart and also it has a stinger that can kill you, oh and it's got big spider legs and tentacles coming out of it, and it's evil and it's gonna kill you because of how deadly and scary and spooky it is!! also it talks and says a bunch of weird prophecy stuff isn't that spooooky?"

Completely non-sequitur, feels like a bunch of low-tier Creepypastas were printed out and chopped up with a steak knife, mixed together, and transcribed into the article. None of the anomalies are even related to each other, they just feel like "what's the most cliche horror-movie eldritch monster I can think up?" in a clinical tone.

Also,

SCP-058 was first encountered at Site ██ as it came out of [DATA EXPUNGED].

is probably the most vague and shoehorned discovery log I've ever seen, and reeks of cover-up. It just feels like "I have no clue how this thing could possibly come into being so I'll toss in some blackboxes and [DATA EXPUNGED] instead".

No question about it, enthusiastic downvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I feel as if this would make a great boss battle in a video game

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Honestly just seems like another generic "thing that kills you" and the fact that it's sentient isn't really a selling point to me, just seems like filler lazily slapped onto there. Eh, I'm going to downvote this one, I don't like it even remotely.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: *Whimpering in terror*

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: sometimes a heart with striking tentacles is all it needs to be

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Despite being old and having a few problems, this one has and still does keep me up at night in terror for some reason.

item.title: I'm sorry
item.content:encoded: For the record I absolutely loved this one and mean nothing by its, but I'm gonna touch down on some topics that may have already been talked about. Firstly, I have no objections to the idea of feeding… But how did we come up with the whole "cow sacrafice" every 3 days without it communicating anything to us. I just fnd it a bit unexplained. And how exactly does it's containment get changed if it kills randomly and is supposedly so hard to move (safely). I guess thats what Class D's are for. Other than all that Ihink this is a real spooky one; the insane babbling, and voice description being my favorite parts
Creepy and Wonderful. To the 300s with you!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
…Alright, now we really need a tale account of what in the universe happened during March Madness.

Saturn deer.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The nightmare is a dream to the nameless slug

The March Madness bracket was a nightmare to all of the contestants except for Lord Blackwood, who emerged victorious.

that wanders across the minefield

I'm pretty sure one of the combatants was a member of a military and thus may have had mines in his arsenal.

the remains of deers and kings

Not sure about the deer, but Hanged King, anybody?
058 was predicting its own future. It has predicted its defeat at the hands of Lord Blackwood… the only question is, what do its other prophecies mean?
Illuminati confirmed

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The nightmare is a dream to the nameless slug that wanders across minefield and the remains of deer and kings.

I know that this was written first, but this screams Lord Blackwood to me. Maybe they've already met?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is the weirdest idea I've seen on this site. But why does it work so well?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1. Hate this one. Hate it, hate it, hate it.

The genius here is that it's not something you can actually investigate. It literally just showed up one day and they were lucky enough to contain it. It doesn't communicate, doesn't hunger, doesn't thirst, it just kills. It only exists to be horrible.
Normally, someone working with that premise is going to come up with something that either doesn't feel real enough, or a half-assed excuse why it's not better documented. Here, I feel satisfied that anyone who works with it takes it as a granted that research is basically impossible. Just lock it up and hope the door holds long enough to build a stronger door. Well done.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Despite the detailed description of the voice, I always imagined 058 as having Salad Fingers' voice. Am I weird?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"a SCP" may be a change warranting considering. I like it as it is, personally. A bit of grammatical weirdness here and there adds to verisimilitude, I think, 'cause you've gotta remember these documents were typed up by people, and those people had bigger priorities than such niggling issues as proper use of "a/an." That's just my opinion, though.

As to the other two, those are from a transcript of the thing's ranting, verbatim, or as near as may be heard. The thing itself is a murderburning bug-legged cow heart delivering cryptic insanity in a British accent. It may not be speaking with correct grammar, but that's probably the least abnormal thing about it, and transcripts aren't worthwhile if they're not actually telling you what was said.

That said, it's possible that it doesn't mean "minefield" as in a field littered with mines. It could mean "my field." Consider the entire line:

SCP-058: The nightmare is a dream to the nameless slug that wanders across minefield and the remains of deer and kings.

Then consider what it does. Rampages anywhere and everywhere it goes, killing whatever it comes across. So, it could be saying that the world is its field, and the nightmare it inflicts: killing, breaking, burning, is a dream to anything that finds the remains it leaves satisfactory, which a slug might, and slugs aren't generally named.

The other line:

SCP-058: Nightshade is shadows in all honest blinks that sort through the bile of newborn plagues, instant warmth is a mother's milk in dreams before anything was ever evil.

Nightshade doesn't necessarily mean dark of night, and very well might not. It could be describing taking nightshade or a derivative as an intoxicant, to sleep at night (if "blinks" is taken to mean rest), or for suicide. Which makes sense, considering how it goes on to say something roughly like "innocence/ignorance is bliss." I don't know. I have no idea what it's trying to say, or if there even is some kind of point to its words.

But, considering the entire transcript, such as it is, I think it sounds like a traumatized, demented WWI vet who died old, alone, forgotten. Correcting it for grammar, then, when it's not speaking grammatically correctly to begin with but may still have some idea of its own meaning, will only lose that meaning in translation. So, I think the article is as it should be, and those edits aren't necessary.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Some minor edits:

"a SCP" < change to [an SCP] (I don't see "SKIP" as being very clinical)

"wanders across minefield" < either [a minefield] or [minefields]

"Nightshade is shadows" < either [Nightshade is shadow] or [Nightshades are shadows]

item.title: Re: Euclid?
item.content:encoded: Because it presents a large challenge to contain and is capable of causing widespread civilian damage.

item.title: Euclid?
item.content:encoded: Why is this classified as Keter?
